body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
The goal of this PR is to convert grid-mean cloud, precipitation, and hydrometeor quantities into subcolumn quantities that can later be used to calculate radar reflectivity for the CloudSat simulator. 

## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->
- Added cloud subcolumn mask generation from grid-mean cloud fraction.
- Added precipitation subcolumn mask generation.
- Added hydrometeor slicing into subcolumns for cloud liquid, cloud ice, rain, and snow based on the 1M microphysics hydrometeor categories.
- Added precomputed fields needed for the online subcolumn workflow.
- Added tests for the implemented subcolumn and hydrometeor logic.


<!---
Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/

- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

-->

- [ ] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: COSP subcolumn

## Purpose 
Per-subdomain SGS hyperdiffusion with the dry-static-energy + water-enthalpy split for mseʲ. Reverses the previous unification with grid-mean tendencies.
